key_finding: L* is causally strong (Cause=0.86, pass) but not attribute-isolated (Isolate=0.39, fail). Leakage is exclusively downstream (L3 semantic probe, 0.17) — not upstream. Phoneme (L1, 0.010) and speaker (L0, 0.001) probes show near-zero leakage. This is expected residual-stream behavior: steering at L* propagates to all l>L* layers. True RAVEL isolation requires either (a) a projection-based steering that stays in the orthogonal complement of non-target directions, or (b) restricting steering to subspaces that don't overlap with semantic representations at L3+.
implications: Cause score 0.86 confirms L* is THE commit layer — steering here reliably shifts AND-frac
Isolate failure is architectural (residual stream), not random — only downstream probes affected
Phoneme and speaker identity (upstream probes) fully protected — L* doesn't retroactively change early representations
Semantic bleed to L3 is a real concern for targeted interventions — need projection-based steering
Paper angle: RAVEL analysis reveals commit direction has strong causal but imperfect attributional specificity; propose orthogonal-complement steering as fix
next_action: Q206 (hallucination suppression) or implement projected steering variant to improve Isolate score
